It goes back to some experiments that I remember reading about and it was an experiment where a lot of scientists and a lot of doctors have discovered that one of the main reasons why a lot of children in today's world have peanut allergies is because of a lack of exposure to peanuts when they were young and maybe their parents didn't even eat peanuts when they were being in mom's belly. As a result of a lack of exposure to peanuts, it's created generations of children now who are allergic to peanuts. To me, I think the same thing is happening now currently in our society where we are so absorbed by these devices, we are so engaged and addicted to screens that we no longer socially interact on a daily basis, on a weekly basis, sometimes on a monthly basis. As a result of spending more time interacting with people online on our phones as opposed to socially in-person interacting, we've created the same situation with peanut allergies. So if someone's afraid of heights, a psychologist would take them through exposure therapy. So if you're afraid of heights and they'll take you to level 0.5 and then they'll kind of get you to experience that and help you realize there's nothing to fear, then they'll take you to level one, then maybe we'll stay at level one for a couple of weeks, then we'll get a level two, then maybe we'll stay there for a couple of weeks and again it's a form of exposure therapy.
